user_prompt: src/main.cpp:13:24: warning: ignoring attribute 'section (".rtc.data.2")' because it conflicts with previous 'section (".rtc.data.0")' [-Wattributes]
src/main.cpp:14:24: warning: ignoring attribute 'section (".rtc.data.3")' because it conflicts with previous 'section (".rtc.data.1")' [-Wattributes]
can you explain what the warnings mean? I was told that RTC_DATA_ATTR makes it store it in a slow persistent RAM region that is kept between resets if power is not lost
agent_reply: Yes, your understanding of `RTC_DATA_ATTR` is basically right: on ESP32 it places the variable into RTC memory, which can survive many reset types as long as power is not removed.
The warning is about applying `RTC_DATA_ATTR` in two places:

`RTC_DATA_ATTR` expands to a GCC section attribute, something like “put this symbol in `.rtc.data...`”. GCC is seeing one section attribute on the `extern` declaration and then another section attribute on the actual definition. Because ESP’s macro expansion gives them distinct internal section names, GCC says: “these declarations disagree, so I’m ignoring one of the attributes.”

The important practical bit: the variables are still likely ending up in RTC memory because the actual definitions in `main.cpp` have `RTC_DATA_ATTR`. But the header declarations should not carry that attribute. The clean pattern is:
So the warning does not mean RTC RAM does not work. It means the attribute was redundantly attached to both the declaration and definition, and GCC is complaining about the mismatch.

So the warning does not mean RTC RAM does not work. It means the attribute was redundantly attached to both the declaration and definition, and GCC is complaining about the mismatch.

user_prompt: sorry for the interruption, you didn't think anything wrong but I wanted to add stuff

at the bottom of `src\all_init.cpp`, add a function called `show_fatal_error_f` that works like printf in terms of calling, include a ESP_LOGE inside, but it also makes the gui run an ErrorView (you may need to create this), all it does is show the error icon (`sprit_error_large`) in the top center of the screen, and then starting from (4, 120), use a font about 12 pixels tall to write the error message out. Word wrap is important.
have a boolean switch for the view to say if it is fatal or if it can be dismissed by touching the screen, and another message line to state so.
apply this to all the boot up messages, the ones that load the files are non-fatal but need a warning

the showing of this error screen is blocking, the `show_fatal_error_f` should have an internal loop that calls the gui poll function. Initialization does not proceed unless the user dismisses the error
agent_reply: Implemented the blocking startup error screen.
- Added `show_fatal_error_f(bool fatal, const char* format, ...)` at the bottom of [all_init.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/all_init.cpp).
- Fatal: NVS, microSD init, AudioManager init, BluetoothManager init, main/splash view failure.
- Dismissible warnings: Bluetooth host list load and Wi-Fi config load.
- Writes wrapped message text from `(4, 120)` with ~12px text.
- Fatal errors block forever with “Fatal error. Restart required.”
- Nonfatal warnings block until touch, then restore the previous view and continue startup.

user_prompt: move `enum class MemoType : uint8_t` to `defs.h`, and we need a to-string function for it inside `lib\UtilFuncs\utilfuncs.cpp`
agent_reply: Moved `MemoType` into [defs.h](/D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/defs.h), updated `MemoTypeButton` to use that shared enum, and added:
The strings are `note`, `todo`, `journal`, `idea`, `reminder`, with `unknown` as the default fallback.
Verified with `pio run -e m5stack-core2`; build passes.

user_prompt: I want to run `void test_sdcard()` to test performance of the recorder with encryption enabled
make sure everything is wired so that it works with encryption actually built and active
agent_reply: Wired `test_sdcard()` so it should actually test encrypted recorder performance now.
- `setup()` now calls `all_init()` before bring-up tests, so NVS/M5/gui basics exist before `Aegis` and SD setup.
- installs a deterministic test master key only if no key already exists,
- forces `AudioFileRecorder::setPurePcmMode(false)` so it does not bypass encryption.
- `AudioFileRecorder` now includes `Aegis` by relative path so PlatformIO library discovery works.
- `BtHostList.h` and `WifiManager.h` now include `conf.h`, so their security-mode structs match their `.cpp` files.
Build passed. I restored the generated `src/version.c` bump afterward.

- `BtHostList.h` and `WifiManager.h` now include `conf.h`, so their security-mode structs match their `.cpp` files.
Build passed. I restored the generated `src/version.c` bump afterward.

user_prompt: the test didn't trigger, what did I do wrong?
agent_reply: You put `TEST_MOCK_FW_UPDATE` in `inc/conf.h`, but [DiskStats.cpp](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/DiskStats.cpp:392) does not include `conf.h` or `thefly_common.h`.
compiled as the non-mock path in `DiskStats.cpp`, meaning boot still required a real `/firmware.bin` on the SD card before showing the dialog.
`FirmwareUpdate.cpp` does see the macro because `FirmwareUpdate.h` includes `thefly_common.h`, but the trigger gate happens earlier in `DiskStats`.
Fix: add this near the top of `DiskStats.cpp`:
One more gotcha: if the dialog shows but the Go button does not, that’s the battery gate. `battery_fullish_for_firmware_update()` currently requires `BattTracker::ChargeLevel::high`.

user_prompt: the encrypted block size might be too small, so the overhead is too large
change it to 8192, make sure this is set inside `inc\conf.h`, with a comment saying that this is approximately 128ms worth of data
agent_reply: Done. The encrypted plaintext block size is now configured in [inc/conf.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/conf.h:51) as:
// 8192 bytes is approximately 128ms worth of 16 kHz 16-bit stereo PCM data.
I removed the hardcoded `1024` from [inc/defs.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/defs.h:17), and `defs.h` now includes `conf.h` directly so the derived encrypted chunk sizes stay self-contained.

user_prompt: I want a master error reporting function
start files `inc\error_report.h` and `src\error_report.cpp`, and for the header, in addition to just function prototypes, it will have convenience macros so not all calls need to use all the args.
the signature should be `bool error_f(thefly_error_t, bool blocking, int16_t next_view, const char* tag, const char* format_str, ...);`
if the error is fatal, it is blocking even though there's a blocking arg
`next_view` is negative if we want it to be automatic
if it is blocking, ignore the `next_view` arg
if it is blocking or fatal, use `ErrorView`, otherwise it is a `ModalDialog` with the appropriate dismissal view handoff.
Serial port is used directly, forget `DBG_LOG*` calls. These are always active.
The dialog don't show `tag` but the serial port message does. `tag` is allowed to be null.
The dialog don't show time but the serial port message does, use ClockAgent for time if it ready.
All the `show_fatal_error_f` inside `setup()` and `all_init()` should be renamed to `show_boot_error_f` via a macro.
do this up to here, do not modify any other call sites for showing error yet, I will need to review each one by one first
agent_reply: Implemented the first pass of the master error reporter.
- direct `Serial.printf()` reporting with type, optional tag, and ClockAgent time when synced

I moved the old `show_fatal_error_f(...)` implementation into the new error module as a compatibility wrapper, so the unreviewed runtime call sites still compile and still behave in the old blocking/ErrorView style.

Boot-path calls in `setup()` and the splash boot failure in [src/all_init.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/all_init.cpp) now use `show_boot_error_f(...)`. I also added `get_view_error()` so `error_report.cpp` can drive the existing `ErrorView`.
Build passed. Note: the build regenerated `src/version.c`, as usual.
